title: Choose Storage
prompt: For each data need, choose a storage pattern and justify it: one API key, 50,000 event logs per day, editorial "case study" records, cached remote API results, and per-user preferences.
hints: The goal is not one storage answer for everything. Match shape to API.

model_answer.summary: Choose storage by data shape, query pattern, retention, and ownership instead of putting everything in one convenient place.
model_answer.work: Store one API key in an option, ideally autoload=no if it is not needed on every request. Store 50,000 event logs per day in a custom table with a primary key, indexes for the query pattern, schema versioning, and a retention cleanup plan. Store editorial case study records as a custom post type because editors get admin screens, revisions, status, and familiar workflows. Store cached remote API results in a transient or persistent object cache entry because cache can disappear and is not durable truth. Store per-user preferences in user meta because the data belongs to a user object.
checks: Verify high-volume event queries use an index and have a retention policy.
Confirm the API key option is not autoloaded unnecessarily.
Smoke test custom post type editorial workflows for case studies.
Test transient expiry and cache miss behavior for remote API results.
Confirm user preference reads and writes are scoped to the correct user.
model_answer.why_this_is_good: It makes each storage choice explainable to a future maintainer and shows why access pattern matters more than convenience.
common_misses: Using one option for high-volume records.
Missing indexes and retention for event logs.
Using custom tables for editorial content that wants WordPress admin behavior.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by adding the owner, query pattern, and retention/migration concern for each data type.

summary.description: One or two sentences explaining the answer.

work.description: The main implementation, review, or design answer.

checks.description: Verification steps, review checks, or acceptance criteria.
